author: Jared Wray
message:
net - chore: upgrade undici to 8 (breaking) (#1682)

undici ^7.24.5 -> ^8.6.0 (@cacheable/net dependency).

No code changes required. @cacheable/net uses undici only for its types
(RequestInit, Response) and re-exports RequestInit as FetchRequestInit;
runtime fetching uses Node's global fetch (to keep instanceof checks aligned
with Node's bundled undici). net's source type-checks cleanly against
undici 8 (verified with tsc --noEmit, since net builds with tsdown which
doesn't type-check), and its 212 tests pass at 100%.

engines.node ">=18", so an engine-strict install on Node 18/19 could fail.

author: Jared Wray
message:
mono - chore: upgrade hookified to 3 (breaking) (#1679)

* mono - chore: upgrade hookified to 3 (breaking)

Upgrade hookified to ^3.0.1 across the 5 packages that use it (cacheable,
flat-cache, @cacheable/memory, @cacheable/net, @cacheable/node-cache).

hookified 3 removed the `Hook` type and changed `onHook` to return
`IHook | undefined`. Updated packages/cacheable/src/index.ts: `Hook` ->
`HookFn`, and the `onHook` overloads now return `IHook | undefined`
(returning super's result). Verified with `tsc --noEmit` since the tsdown
build does not type-check source.

hookified 2 also flipped the `throwOnEmptyListeners` default from false to
true. The getOrSet(..., { cacheErrors: true }) path emits an "error" event
(via @cacheable/utils) with no listener, which now throws instead of caching
the error. Restored `throwOnEmptyListeners: false` in the 4 packages moving
from hookified 1.x (cacheable, flat-cache, @cacheable/memory, @cacheable/net)
to preserve prior behavior. node-cache was already on 2.x and is unaffected.

hookified 3 requires Node >=22.18; the repo is on Node 24 and CI's 22 is
22.18+, so no change needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01P1TBuMiXotBjpNHYcbwvqf

* node-cache - chore: align engines.node with hookified 3

hookified@3.0.1 requires node >=22.18.0, but @cacheable/node-cache declared
engines.node ">=22", so an engine-strict install on Node 22.0-22.17 could
fail. Raise the floor to ">=22.18.0" to match the dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01P1TBuMiXotBjpNHYcbwvqf

author: Jared Wray
message:
mono - chore: upgrade TypeScript to 6 (breaking) (#1672)

Upgrade the TypeScript compiler 5.9.3 -> 6.0.3 across all 9 packages that
declare it.

TS 6.0 requires an explicit rootDir when the output layout depends on it
(TS5011); this surfaced in cacheable-request's tsc-based build. Added
rootDir "./src" to its tsconfig.build.json -- behavior-preserving, it makes
explicit what TypeScript previously inferred.

The tsdown-built packages (which still use the now-deprecated
moduleResolution "node" / baseUrl) build cleanly: those options don't error
under TS 6 via tsdown, so they're left as-is for a separate TS 7-prep
cleanup rather than expanding this upgrade.

- typescript ^5.9.3 -> ^6.0.3 (9 packages)
- cacheable-request/tsconfig.build.json: add rootDir "./src"

author: Jared Wray
message:
test(cacheable-request): replace sqlite3 test dependency with mock store (#1668)

* test(cacheable-request): replace sqlite3 test dependency with mock store

cacheable-request pulled in sqlite3 and @keyv/sqlite as devDependencies
solely to exercise two cache-connection error paths in its tests
(CacheError emission and automaticFailover fallback). sqlite3 is a heavy
native module: it needed an allowBuilds entry and carried two copies in
the lockfile (6.0.1 directly + 5.1.7 transitively via @keyv/sqlite).

Replace the two KeyvSqlite("sqlite://non/existent/database.sqlite") usages
with a lightweight in-test mock store whose operations reject with a
SQLITE_CANTOPEN-coded error. This preserves the exact coverage (the error
event path was already covered separately via a plain Keyv instance)
without a native dependency. SQLite remains a fully supported storage
adapter for users of the library.

- Drop @keyv/sqlite and sqlite3 devDependencies and the testdb.sqlite
  path from the clean script
- Remove the sqlite3 allowBuilds entry and the testdb.sqlite .gitignore line
- Regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_011PoyCZFLpx5BXNih6jNiaQ

* test(cacheable-request): make mock store async and drop any cast

Address review feedback on the unopenable mock store: make its operations
async so they reject a Promise (mirroring a real async Keyv adapter), and
use Object.assign to attach the SQLITE_CANTOPEN code instead of an `any`
cast. No behavior or coverage change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(net): correct caching claims and standalone helper usage in README

Apply accuracy fixes found via source verification and PR review:
- Only GET is cached by default and only PUT supports per-request
  caching (caching: true); POST/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD are never cached at
  the fetch layer, so the caching option has no effect on them
- Note that PUT cache identity is method + URL only (body is not part
  of the cache key)
- Scope request coalescing to simple caching mode and WHOIS/RDAP
  lookups (the default RFC 7234 path does not coalesce)
- Clarify that standalone get/post/patch/head require an options
  argument and del requires an options object (they throw otherwise)

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat: registry-aware release workflow with provenance + OIDC (#1664)

* feat: add registry-aware release workflow with provenance + OIDC

Add a release pipeline that publishes only the workspace packages whose
manually-set version is ahead of the npm registry — versions are never
bumped by the tooling.

- scripts/release.mjs: enumerates publishable packages (pnpm), compares
  each local version against the registry (404/new/already-published),
  computes the full plan before publishing, and publishes in dependency
  (topological) order using pnpm only with provenance. Supports --dry-run
  (plan + `pnpm publish --dry-run` packaging validation), --json, and
  writes a GitHub step summary + outputs.
- .github/workflows/release.yml: runs on push to main (package.json
  changes) and manual dispatch with a dry-run input; grants id-token:
  write for OIDC trusted publishing + provenance, with NPM_TOKEN as an
  optional fallback.
- RELEASE.md: documents the flow and the one-time npmjs.com trusted
  publisher setup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

* fix: address review feedback on release tooling

- release.mjs: encode all "/" in scoped package names for the registry URL
  (replaceAll) — fixes the CodeQL "incomplete string escaping" alert.
- release.mjs: abort a real publish on the first failure so a failed
  dependency never leaves dependents publishing against a missing version;
  a dry run still validates every package. Report packages skipped by the
  abort.
- release.mjs: add a 10s AbortSignal.timeout to registry fetches so a
  stalled registry fails fast into the existing retry/backoff.
- release.yml: set persist-credentials: false on checkout (the job only
  reads the repo and never pushes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

* refactor: make release fully tokenless (OIDC trusted publishing only)

Remove the NODE_AUTH_TOKEN/NPM_TOKEN fallback so publishing relies solely
on OIDC trusted publishing, eliminating the long-lived npm token. Each
package must have a trusted publisher configured on npmjs.com; document
that a brand-new package's first publish needs a one-time manual publish.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

* fix: guard against version rollbacks and off-main manual publishes

Addresses two Codex review findings:

- release.mjs: refuse to publish a package whose local version is behind
  the registry's `latest` dist-tag. Publishing under the default tag would
  move `latest` (and every `^`/`~` consumer) backward, so an accidental
  rollback now fails the run before anything is published. Adds a small
  spec-correct semver comparator (no new dependency).
- release.yml: force a dry run for any manual dispatch from a ref other
  than `main`, so branch versions can't be published to npm (the publish
  step uses --no-git-checks). Real publishing only happens on main.

The "abort on first publish failure" finding was already handled in the
prior review-fixes commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

* fix: trigger release on published GitHub release, not push to main

Per review: the release workflow should run when a GitHub release is
published (in lockstep with deploy-website.yml, which also runs on
`released`) or on manual dispatch — not on every push to main.

- Replace the push-to-main trigger with `release: types: [released]`.
- A published release publishes for real; a manual run still honors its
  dry-run input and is forced to a dry run from any ref other than main.
- Update RELEASE.md to describe the release-driven flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

* docs: move release docs into CONTRIBUTING.md

Per review: fold the release process into the existing CONTRIBUTING.md as
a "Releasing" section instead of a standalone RELEASE.md file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

* docs: add Releasing section to CONTRIBUTING.md

Completes the move from RELEASE.md (the prior commit deleted the file but
the staging of CONTRIBUTING.md was dropped due to a git add error).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F7cjEDzGAPQUmvQehU1sGF

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(cacheable): add getStaticInstance shared singleton accessor (#1665)

* feat(cacheable): add getStaticInstance shared singleton accessor

Add a static `Cacheable.getStaticInstance(options?)` accessor that lazily
creates and returns a single shared instance, so callers can use one global
cache without constructing and passing an instance around. Options are applied
only when the instance is first created; later calls return the existing
instance.

Also add `Cacheable.setStaticInstance(instance?)` to replace the shared
instance or clear it with `undefined` (the reset path, useful for tests and
reconfiguring).

Includes unit tests covering identity, first-call options, options ignored
after creation, replacement, and reset, plus a new README section and API
entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01JastxGGo1xPEJFy8N9YBpe

* feat(cacheable): emit error when getStaticInstance is re-configured

Address PR review feedback. When getStaticInstance(options) is called with
options after the shared instance already exists, the options cannot reconfigure
the existing instance. Instead of silently ignoring them, emit a
CacheableEvents.ERROR on the instance to surface the conflict, while still
returning the existing instance. This routes the conflict through the library's
standard error-event channel rather than throwing, keeping the accessor
non-fatal and consistent with how Cacheable reports other errors.

Also document the CommonJS/ESM caveat: because the package ships separate CJS and
ESM builds, an app that loads both formats gets one shared instance per build.

Update the unit test to assert the error is emitted, and refresh the README
section and API entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01JastxGGo1xPEJFy8N9YBpe

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(memory): strongly-typed onHook and hooks documentation (#1663)

* feat(memory): strongly-typed onHook and hooks documentation

Bring @cacheable/memory hooks to parity with cacheable:

- Add a strongly-typed onHook overload backed by a new
  CacheableMemoryHookHandlerMap so built-in CacheableMemoryHooks names
  infer their handler payloads (with a loose Hookified fallback for
  custom events).
- Export hook payload types: CacheableMemoryHookItem,
  CacheableMemoryAfterGetItem, and CacheableMemoryAfterGetManyItem.
- Document the 14 hooks, usage, and the typed API in the README.
- Add a test covering the exported payload types via onHook.

The hooks themselves already existed and fire via hookSync; this adds
type safety and docs without changing runtime behavior. Coverage
remains 100%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0141FhENX9QqnUDzKkZomECo

* fix(memory): accurate AFTER_GET_MANY type and async-hook docs

Address review feedback on the memory hooks types/docs:

- CacheableMemoryAfterGetManyItem.result is now Array<T | undefined>:
  getMany pushes the get() result per key, which is undefined for
  missing/expired keys, so the payload can contain undefined.
- Clarify that hookSync skips async handler functions entirely (they do
  not run at all), rather than running them un-awaited, in both the
  README and the handler-map JSDoc.
- Extend the typed-payload test to cover a getMany miss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0141FhENX9QqnUDzKkZomECo

* refactor(memory): drop custom onHook overload, use inherited Hookified.onHook

Per review: no need to force the hook types with a custom onHook override.
CacheableMemory now relies on the inherited Hookified.onHook, and the
CacheableMemoryHookHandlerMap has been removed. The hook payload types
(CacheableMemoryHookItem, CacheableMemoryAfterGetItem,
CacheableMemoryAfterGetManyItem) remain exported as optional annotation
helpers, and the README/enum docs note the sync-only dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0141FhENX9QqnUDzKkZomECo

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(memory): add statistics tracking via @cacheable/utils Stats (#1662)

* feat(memory): add statistics tracking via @cacheable/utils Stats

Add an opt-in `stats` option to `CacheableMemory` that tracks runtime
statistics (hits, misses, gets, sets, deletes, clears, count, ksize,
vsize) using the shared `Stats` implementation from `@cacheable/utils`,
consistent with `cacheable` and `@cacheable/node-cache`.

- Add `stats?: boolean` option and a `stats` getter exposing the Stats
  instance; statistics are disabled by default.
- Instrument the leaf operations (`get`, `getRaw`, `set`, `delete`,
  `clear`) so batch and derived methods (`getMany`, `setMany`,
  `deleteMany`, `has`, `take`, `wrap`, `getOrSet`) inherit tracking.
- Keep `count`/`ksize`/`vsize` accurate on overwrite and on delete, and
  reset them on `clear`.
- Re-export `Stats`, `StatsOptions`, and `StatsSnapshot`.
- Add full test coverage (100%) and document the feature in the README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_012LnVP8LHiL2fPi3BycCYWY

* fix(memory): keep size stats in sync when expired entries are removed

Lazy expiration removed entries directly from the store, so the size
counters (count, ksize, vsize) were never decremented and drifted
upward permanently. Add a recordExpiration helper and call it at every
expiration-removal site (get, getRaw, the keys/items getters, and
checkExpiration). Expirations decrement the size stats but are not
counted as deletes, since they are not user-initiated.

Also simplify a duplicate `item.expires && item.expires` check in
getRaw, update the get/getRaw expiry tests to assert the size stats are
decremented, and add tests for the keys/items/checkExpiration paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_012LnVP8LHiL2fPi3BycCYWY

* fix(memory,utils): keep size stats consistent on late-enable and store resize

Addresses two edge cases where the size counters could desync from the
cache contents:

- Clamp Stats.decreaseVSize / decreaseKSize / decreaseCount at 0 so the
  counters never go negative when statistics are enabled after the cache
  already holds (untracked) entries that are later deleted or overwritten.
- Reset the size stats in CacheableMemory's storeHashSize setter, which
  recreates the underlying stores and clears all entries.

Add tests for both paths in @cacheable/utils and @cacheable/memory, and
document the enable-before-populate / storeHashSize behavior in the README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_012LnVP8LHiL2fPi3BycCYWY

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(memory): add getOrSet cache-aside helper to CacheableMemory (#1661)

* feat(memory): add getOrSet cache-aside helper to CacheableMemory

CacheableMemory had `wrap` but no `getOrSet`, even though the package
README already linked to a "Get Or Set Memoization Function" section that
did not exist. This adds the synchronous cache-aside helper.

- Add `getOrSetSync` (+ `GetOrSetSyncOptions` / `GetOrSetSyncKey`) to
  `@cacheable/utils`, mirroring the async `getOrSet` error semantics
  (`cacheErrors`, `throwErrors` with `function`/`store` scoping). Sync
  execution is inherently coalesced, so no stampede protection is needed.
- Add `CacheableMemory.getOrSet(key, fn, options?)` backed by
  `getOrSetSync`; writes go through `set` so `maxTtl` capping and
  BEFORE/AFTER_SET hooks still apply.
- Re-export the new util and types from `@cacheable/memory`.
- Fill in the previously dead README section documenting `getOrSet`.
- Tests for both packages (100% coverage on @cacheable/memory).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01CThksx3yULdnqQmPF5epua

* fix(utils): don't let error-caching bypass throwErrors in getOrSet/getOrSetSync

A code review flagged that when `cacheErrors` is enabled and the value was
computed but the store write failed (a "store" error), the catch block would
try to cache that error with another `set` to the same unhealthy store. That
second write throws, and because it was unguarded it escaped the function as an
uncaught throw/rejection — bypassing the `throwErrors` contract entirely (even
when `throwErrors` is `false` or `"function"`).

Fix both the async `getOrSet` and the new sync `getOrSetSync` identically (they
share the same error-handling shape):
- Only cache errors that originate in the value function (`errorType ===
  "function"`); a failed store write has no error worth caching.
- Wrap the error-caching `set` in try/catch and emit any secondary failure
  instead of letting it propagate.

Adds regression tests to both for the store-write-fails and
error-cache-write-fails paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01CThksx3yULdnqQmPF5epua

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(cacheable): support per-store TTL overrides via hooks (#1657)

* feat(cacheable): support per-store TTL overrides via hooks

Allow a BEFORE_SET hook to assign a per-store object ({ primary,
secondary }) to item.ttl so the primary and secondary stores can be given
different expirations, mirroring the existing operation-level per-store
TTL. The hook item now uses a tracked ttl accessor, so any assignment
(even the same value) counts as an override; an omitted field of a
per-store object falls back to that store's normal cascade. After
resolution item.ttl is normalized to the effective primary number so
AFTER_SET handlers and sync replication always observe a number.

Extend the same per-store support to setMany / BEFORE_SET_MANY and the
operation-level setMany item ttl. Tag snapshots use the longer of the two
store TTLs (consolidated into a shared maxStoreTtl helper) so a later
invalidation can always reach the longest-lived copy.

Fully document the override precedence, maxTtl capping, AFTER_SET / sync
normalization, and backfill behavior in the README. Add a CacheableHookItem
type for opt-in hook handler typing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_018gcwQAcwjCjFP2Myh8kCMz

* fix(cacheable): address review feedback on per-store TTL hooks

- Guard the BEFORE_SET object branch against null (typeof null === "object")
  so a hook clearing ttl with null no longer cascades to store defaults.
- Treat a ttl of 0 as immortal when sizing tag snapshots, so a snapshot
  cannot expire while an immortal (ttl 0) store copy is still cached.
- Publish the effective (cascaded + capped) primary ttl from setMany sync,
  not just the raw per-store field, so subscribers stay consistent when an
  item omits the primary field. Add a shared resolveStoreTtl helper used by
  setManyKeyv, setManyKeyTags, and the sync publish.
- v8-ignore the fire-and-forget non-blocking setMany secondary error path,
  matching the existing sibling patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_018gcwQAcwjCjFP2Myh8kCMz

* feat(cacheable): typed onHook map + hook review fixes

- Add a strongly-typed onHook overload mapping each CacheableHooks name to
  its payload (BEFORE_SET/AFTER_SET -> CacheableHookItem, BEFORE_SET_MANY ->
  CacheableSetItem[], BEFORE_SECONDARY_SETS_PRIMARY -> scalar-ttl item, etc.),
  with a loose fallback for arbitrary events. Hook handlers now autocomplete
  and type-check (e.g. assigning a per-store object in
  BEFORE_SECONDARY_SETS_PRIMARY is a compile error). Export the new payload
  types (CacheableAfterGetItem, CacheableAfterGetManyItem,
  CacheableSecondarySetsPrimaryItem) and CacheableHookHandlerMap.
- Guard BEFORE_SECONDARY_SETS_PRIMARY: resolve a per-store object / shorthand
  down to the primary value before writing the primary store, so a JS caller
  can't hand Keyv a non-numeric ttl.
- Publish the in-scope effective primary number to sync from set() instead of
  the widened item.ttl accessor (fixes a latent body type error).
- Document the TTL precedence as one canonical table, and the hook sharp edges:
  0/null/undefined clears the ttl (immortal), an invalid shorthand aborts the
  whole set, AFTER_SET sees a number while AFTER_SET_MANY sees the item as
  passed, and how per-store ttl interacts with sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_018gcwQAcwjCjFP2Myh8kCMz

* fix(cacheable): treat negative/NaN ttl as no ttl

Floor invalid per-operation and per-hook TTLs in capTtl: a negative or NaN
ttl is normalized to "no ttl" (parity with the instance-level > 0 guards in
setTtl/setMaxTtl) instead of flowing into keyv.set as an immediate/NaN
expiry. A ttl of 0 is preserved since it means the entry never expires.
Covers both set() and setMany since they resolve effective per-store TTLs
through capTtl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_018gcwQAcwjCjFP2Myh8kCMz

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
refactor(utils): expose Stats per-key data as public `trackedKeys` map (#1660)

* refactor(utils): expose Stats per-key data as public `trackedKeys` map

Rename the private `_keyCounts` map to a public `trackedKeys` field so
consumers can read per-key counters (and `trackedKeys.size`) directly,
and drop the now-redundant `trackedKeyCount` getter. Export the
`KeyCounters` value type and update the tests and README accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01SjsBvVfhc7vKeqPE4nWCUr

* refactor(utils): expose Stats.trackedKeys as a read-only map getter

Address review feedback: keep the per-key map private (`_trackedKeys`)
and expose it through a public getter returning
`ReadonlyMap<string, Readonly<KeyCounters>>`. Consumers keep direct
`trackedKeys.size` / `.get()` access while no longer being able to mutate
internal state and bypass pruning and `maxTrackedKeys` invariants.

author: Jared Wray
message:
docs(cacheable): correct primary/secondary iteration guidance (#1659)

The "Iteration on Primary and Secondary Stores" section implied the
default in-memory primary store could be walked with
`cache.primary.iterator()`. Keyv only assigns `iterator()` when the
underlying store is a raw `Map` or a recognized iterable adapter
(redis, valkey, mongo, sqlite, postgres, mysql, etcd); the default
`@cacheable/memory` primary (`createKeyv()`) is neither, so
`cache.primary.iterator` is `undefined` and the old example silently
iterated nothing.

Rewrite the section to:
- explain exactly when Keyv exposes `iterator()` and that it is
  `undefined` otherwise (always feature-check),
- show secondary-store iteration (the common Redis/Valkey case),
- show how to get an iterable primary via a Map-backed `new Keyv()`,
- document iterating the default in-memory primary through
  `CacheableMemory.items` (including the Keyv `{ value, expires }`
  envelope, so the decoded value is `item.value.value`),
- correct the storage-adapter support list.

All examples were verified empirically against the actual Keyv and
@cacheable/memory behavior.


Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BCV53CPzQxWNWghd39QfVd

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(net): add WHOIS and RDAP lookups to @cacheable/net (#1658)

* feat(net): add WHOIS and RDAP lookups to @cacheable/net

Add cached WHOIS (TCP port 43) and RDAP (HTTPS/JSON) lookups for domains,
IPv4/IPv6 addresses, and ASNs with both raw and parsed JSON output.

- whois(): traditional WHOIS over node:net returning raw text plus parsed
  fields, with dynamic IANA server discovery, registry -> registrar referral
  following (with loop/depth protection), and a generic key/value parser.
- whoisRaw(): convenience returning only the raw text.
- rdap(): RDAP lookups resolved via the IANA bootstrap registries (fetched
  through the package's cached fetch), returning the parsed object and raw JSON.
- CacheableNet.whois()/rdap() methods using the instance cache, plus standalone
  functions and exported helpers (queryWhoisServer, parseWhois,
  normalizeWhoisQuery, detectQueryType).
- Server discovery and results are cached via the existing Cacheable instance,
  and concurrent identical lookups are coalesced.

Tested with local TCP/HTTP fake servers at 100% coverage; no new runtime deps
(WHOIS uses node:net/node:url, RDAP reuses the existing fetch/undici).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0153VmEzvkNSLQCcNLhhEs5h

* fix(net): harden WHOIS/RDAP against ReDoS, prototype pollution, and malformed input

Address CodeQL and review feedback on the WHOIS/RDAP lookups:

- whois normalize: replace the polynomial `/\.+$/` trailing-dot regex (CodeQL
  ReDoS) with a non-backtracking endsWith loop.
- parseWhois / merged fields: build results on null-prototype objects so
  untrusted keys ("__proto__", "constructor") cannot pollute the prototype and
  inherited members never interfere with lookups.
- parseReferralValue: fall back to the default port when a referral carries a
  non-numeric port instead of passing NaN to createConnection.
- rdap ipv6ToBigInt: clamp the padding length so a malformed address with more
  than 8 groups cannot trigger a RangeError.
- rdap bootstrap: check response.ok and throw a clear error instead of a cryptic
  JSON parse failure when the IANA bootstrap fetch fails.

Adds regression tests for each; coverage remains 100%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0153VmEzvkNSLQCcNLhhEs5h

* fix(net): address review feedback on WHOIS/RDAP correctness and caching

- whois normalize: strip an authority port (e.g. "example.com:8443") for
  host:port inputs while leaving IPv6 literals intact.
- detectQueryType: recognize a bare numeric ASN (e.g. "15169"), not only the
  AS-prefixed form.
- whois/rdap cache keys: include the result-affecting options (server, port,
  bootstrap config, query prefix, encoding / RDAP headers) so a lookup against
  one server/config never serves a cache hit meant for another.
- rdap: do not forward caller headers (e.g. Authorization) to the public IANA
  bootstrap endpoint.
- rdap: stop caching the final RDAP query at the fetch layer; the rdap:<query>
  result cache owns the TTL, so a GET:<url> entry can no longer outlive and
  bypass the requested ttl.

Adds regression tests for each; coverage remains 100%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0153VmEzvkNSLQCcNLhhEs5h

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
cacheable - feat: support per-store TTL per operation (#1656)

* feat(cacheable): support per-store TTL per operation

Allow the `ttl` on `set`, `getOrSet`, and `wrap` to accept a per-store
object `{ primary?, secondary? }` in addition to a number or shorthand
string. Each field is resolved independently so a single operation can
expire a key at different rates in the primary and secondary stores
(e.g. 10s in memory, 5m in Redis). Fields left undefined fall back to
that store's own default TTL resolution, and a scalar TTL keeps the
existing behavior of applying to both stores.

Adds a `PerStoreTtl` type and `resolvePerStoreTtl` helper to
`@cacheable/utils` and widens the shared option/adapter TTL types so the
object form flows through `getOrSet`/`wrap` to `Cacheable.set`, which is
the only place that interprets it. `maxTtl` continues to cap each store
independently.

Closes #1654

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01HjpVjtgWd5wyw17rsywLb8

* refactor(utils): make resolvePerStoreTtl null/undefined handling explicit

Use an explicit early return for null/undefined inputs instead of
relying on shorthandToMilliseconds's lenient handling. Behavior is
unchanged (both already resolved to undefined), but the contract is now
clearer. Adds a null-input test to keep full coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01HjpVjtgWd5wyw17rsywLb8

* fix(cacheable): keep tag snapshot alive for the longest-lived store copy

With per-store TTLs the secondary copy can be shorter-lived than the
primary. The tag snapshot previously expired with the secondary TTL, so
once the secondary copy lapsed the snapshot was gone while the primary
copy was still cached, and a later tag invalidation could no longer mark
that copy stale. Set the snapshot TTL to the longest-lived store copy
(undefined when either store never expires) so invalidation is honored
for as long as any copy of the value remains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01HjpVjtgWd5wyw17rsywLb8

* refactor(cacheable): keep per-store TTL off shared wrap/getOrSet option types

The per-store TTL object is a Cacheable-only concept (primary/secondary
stores), but widening the shared WrapFunctionOptions/GetOrSetFunctionOptions
in @cacheable/utils also exposed it on single-store consumers such as
CacheableMemory.wrap, where the object is silently ignored.

Revert the public/sync option types in @cacheable/utils to number|string
and widen only the internal WrapOptions/GetOrSetOptions (plus the
CacheInstance adapter) so the object can still flow through to
Cacheable.set. Cacheable now defines its own wide WrapFunctionOptions/
GetOrSetFunctionOptions, so CacheableMemory.wrap rejects a per-store
object at compile time while Cacheable.wrap/getOrSet accept it.

Also document that a per-store primary TTL governs the write only; after
an L1 entry expires, backfill from the secondary follows the existing TTL
propagation rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01HjpVjtgWd5wyw17rsywLb8

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Jared Wray
message:
feat(cacheable): tag-based invalidation via CacheTags (#1655)

* feat(cacheable): add tag-based invalidation with CacheTags

Implements tag-based invalidation for Cacheable (#1640) on top of the
CacheTags service in @cacheable/utils:

- set(key, value, { ttl, tags }) associates entries with tags
  (back-compat with ttl as the third argument)
- setMany items accept per-item tags
- invalidateTag / invalidateTags bump tag versions in constant time
- stale entries are detected lazily on get / getMany, removed from both
  stores, and a delete is published via sync when enabled
- tag metadata lives in the secondary store when configured so
  invalidations are shared across instances; tags: true option lets
  read-only instances honor invalidations
- getTags(key), tags service getter, and tagsEnabled introspection
- utils: add CacheTags.isKeyStale (safe for untagged keys) and
  CacheTags.getKeyTags

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CEUJ2ZvfykEnuUfL43PobY

* perf(cacheable): batch tag snapshot removal and stale-key deletion

Addresses review feedback:
- add CacheTags.removeKeys for batched snapshot deletion via deleteMany
- removeKeyTags now uses a single batched delete instead of per-key deletes
- getManyRaw collects stale keys and removes them with one deleteMany call

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CEUJ2ZvfykEnuUfL43PobY

* refactor(cacheable): make CacheTags self-contained per review feedback

- CacheTags now owns an enabled state: read methods no-op while disabled
  and tag writes (setKeyTags, invalidateTag, invalidateTags) auto-enable
  the service, so integrations are self-contained
- add CacheTags.getStaleKeys for batch staleness checks (two store reads
  total) and use it in getManyRaw
- move non-blocking (fire-and-forget) handling into CacheTags via a
  nonBlocking option on setKeyTags/removeKey/removeKeys with an onError
  callback for failures
- rename getKeyTags to getTags
- Cacheable creates the tag service by default in the constructor as
  _tags, exposes it via the tags getter, and drops the duplicated
  invalidateTag/invalidateTags/getTags/tagsEnabled members in favor of
  cacheable.tags.*

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CEUJ2ZvfykEnuUfL43PobY

* fix(cacheable): require explicit enable for tags, no auto-enable

Tag writes no longer auto-enable the CacheTags service. Auto-enable only
flipped the local instance, which gives inconsistent behavior across
distributed instances sharing a store. Tags must now be enabled
explicitly via the tags: true option or tags.enabled = true on every
instance; while disabled, all tag operations are no-ops.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CEUJ2ZvfykEnuUfL43PobY

* refactor(cacheable): check tags.enabled at call sites before tag operations

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CEUJ2ZvfykEnuUfL43PobY

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
